# UC-11.6.6 · Proofpoint TRAP Threat Response Auto-Pull Quarantine Action Audit

## Description

Threat Response auto-pull removes malicious mail post-delivery; auditing those actions proves what was retracted, by whom or what automation, and supports investigations when users dispute missing messages.

## Value

Strengthens accountability for aggressive remediation, supports insider-threat reviews, and satisfies regulators asking for traceability of mailbox interventions.

## Implementation

(1) Enable quarantine / TRAP audit logging to Splunk. (2) Retain message IDs for correlation to TAP events. (3) Separate human vs system actors. (4) Export weekly digest to IR leadership. (5) Integrate with case management IDs if present.

## SPL

```spl
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint:quarantine" earliest=-7d
| eval action=coalesce(action, Action, remediation_action, "")
| eval actor=coalesce(admin_user, Admin, operator, system_actor, "trap")
| eval mid=coalesce(message_id, MessageID, msg_id, "")
| where match(lower(action), "(?i)pull|delete|remove|remediate|purge|auto")
| stats count values(action) as actions earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen by mid actor
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (message ID, actor, actions, time window), timeline, bar chart (actions per day).

- [Proofpoint — Threat Response](https://www.proofpoint.com/us/products/advanced-threat-protection/threat-response)
